description:
This conceptual grid diagram displays an 8x7 matrix (8 rows, 7 columns) of classified pixels, distinguishing between Persistent Scatterers (PS) and Distributed Scatterers (DS).
There are 4 blue-coloured cells labelled 'PS' (Persistent Scatterers), located at grid coordinates:
- Row 1, Column 5
- Row 3, Column 4
- Row 5, Column 3
- Row 8, Column 7
There are 29 cells labelled 'DS' (Distributed Scatterers), grouped into clusters and distinguished by colour:
- 6 orange 'DS' pixels, forming two clusters: one of three pixels at Row 1 (Columns 1, 2, 3) and another of two pixels at Row 2 (Columns 3, 4), plus one pixel at Row 3 (Column 3).
- 7 green 'DS' pixels, forming two clusters: one of two pixels at Row 5 (Column 2) and Row 6 (Column 2), and another of five pixels at Row 7 (Columns 2, 3, 4) and Row 8 (Columns 2, 3).
- 16 grey 'DS' pixels, forming a larger connected cluster spanning from Row 4 (Column 7) down to Row 8 (Columns 5, 6).
The remaining 23 grid cells are unlabelled and uncoloured, representing empty or unclassified areas. This diagram illustrates a typical output of pixel classification in radar interferometry, where PS are individual stable targets and DS are areas with statistically uniform scattering properties within a pixel window.
